message:
Preserve column order and dtype through subsetting, fix outer join performance, add scalar-to-string casting, and correct numeric boundaries

columnar (fp-columnar):
- Add direct Int64-vs-Int64 comparison path in scalar_compare to avoid
  precision loss from f64 cast for large integers
- Fix float-to-Int64 promotion boundary: use < 9223372036854775808.0
  (2^63) instead of <= i64::MAX-as-f64 which rounds up and allows overflow

frame (fp-frame):
- Preserve original column dtype through sort_values, where_mask, head,
  tail, loc, iloc, and concat_axis1 by using Column::new(dtype, values)
  instead of Column::from_values which re-infers dtype from data
- Detect and reject duplicate column names in rename() with descriptive error
- Replace nightly-only .is_multiple_of(2) with % 2 == 0 for median

groupby (fp-groupby):
- Replace .is_multiple_of(2) with % 2 == 0 for median calculation

index (fp-index):
- Fix Index::slice to clamp start before computing end, preventing
  incorrect end values when start exceeds array length

io (fp-io):
- Preserve original CSV/JSON column ordering by tracking column_order
  and using DataFrame::new_with_column_order instead of relying on
  BTreeMap alphabetical ordering
- Write CSV/JSON columns in DataFrame column order rather than
  BTreeMap key order
- Format Float64/Bool/Null index labels with to_string instead of Debug

join (fp-join):
- Replace O(n) linear .contains() scans with O(1) hash map lookups for
  checking right-side unmatched labels in outer joins across
  estimate_output_rows, join_series (global allocator and arena), and
  merge_dataframes_on_with_options

types (fp-types):
- Fix float-to-Int64 cast upper bound (same 2^63 boundary fix)
- Support Bool/Int64/Float64 to Utf8 casting via to_string instead of
  returning InvalidCast
- Replace .is_multiple_of(2) with % 2 == 0 in nanmedian

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
